display.purpose: Largest titles; rare in this product-register tool.

body-compact.purpose: The workhorse for dense surfaces: tables, rule criteria, property panels.

label.purpose: Eyebrow captions, column headers, badge text. Uppercase allowed only here, max 4 words.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Resting lift for a detached card or the open state of an expandable row.

shadows.name: elevation
shadows.purpose: Product floating surfaces (search bar, floating panels); navy-tinted double shadow.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single action button. Interactive Blue fill, deepens to brand navy on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #007ab3; color: #fff; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Allianz Neo', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #003781; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #008ed6; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Low-emphasis action: white fill, blue text and border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #fff; color: #007ab3; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #007ab3; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'Allianz Neo', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #f1f9fa; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #008ed6; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Read-only status pill. Always carries a label, never color alone. Shown here in success (Accept).
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 100px; font-family: 'Allianz Neo', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 16px; } .ds-badge--success { background: #1e8927; color: #fff; }

components.name: Card
components.description: White surface on a grey page. Flat at rest, 1px subtle border, 4px radius.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; color: #121212; border: 1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius: 4px; padding: 24px; font-family: 'Allianz Neo', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: White field, subtle border, blue focus border + ring.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="Search for a buyer" />
components.css: .ds-input { background: #fff; color: #121212; border: 1px solid #d9d9d9; border-radius: 4px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: 'Allianz Neo', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #767676; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #008ed6; outline-offset: 1px; border-color: #007ab3; }

narrative.overview: Qirin is a working surface for credit professionals, not a showroom. It carries the calm authority of an Allianz Trade underwriter's desk: everything needed is in front of you, arranged so the eye finds the constraint that matters without hunting. The system earns trust through rigor (consistent spacing, a single confident blue, status that always says what it means) never through decoration. Density is deliberate: a screen can hold a 14-criteria rule or a full buyer dossier and still read in one pass, because hierarchy (scale, weight, whitespace) does the sorting, not the user. It rejects the generic-AI-SaaS look, the austere gray-on-gray spreadsheet, and anything consumer or playful.
keyCharacteristics: One confident brand blue, used where action or identity lives; never sprinkled.
Density with hierarchy: dense is fine, cluttered is not.
Status meaning is never color-only; always paired with a label or icon.
Every value flows from Figma-backed tokens (Foundation to Semantic); no hardcoded styles.
Allianz Neo throughout, weight contrast over multiple families.

rules.name: The One Blue Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one action color (Interactive Blue #007ab3). If something is blue, it is actionable or selected. Informational blue is a different hue (BlueB #496ebd) on purpose; do not blur the two.

rules.name: The Status-Earns-Its-Color Rule
rules.body: The four functional colors are forbidden as decoration. A green element means accepted; a red element means refused/error. Color always rides alongside a label or icon, never alone.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Family Rule
rules.body: Never reach for a second typeface to create hierarchy. Allianz Neo at 400/600/700 plus the size scale covers every level.

rules.name: The Compact-Default Rule
rules.body: On data-dense surfaces, 14px Body Compact is the default, not 16px. Reserve 16px Body for prose and empty states.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest, separated by tone and 1px borders. A shadow appears only when an element floats or responds to state.

dos: Do reference semantic tokens for every color, space, size, and radius (var(--semantic-...)). Foundation to Semantic to component is the only path.
Do keep one action color: Interactive Blue (#007ab3) means actionable/selected, full stop.
Do pair every status color with a label or icon; meaning must survive grayscale and color-blindness.
Do default to 14px Body Compact on dense surfaces; reserve 16px for prose.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest; add shadow only for floating layers or state changes.
Do create hierarchy with Allianz Neo weights (400/600/700) and the size scale.
donts: Don't use cream/sand/beige body backgrounds, decorative gradients, or gradient text.
Don't ship the hero-metric template or endless identical icon+heading+text card grids.
Don't put a tiny uppercase tracked eyebrow above every section, or numbered 01/02/03 section markers as scaffolding.
Don't fall into the austere gray-on-gray dense grid with no hierarchy.
Don't use glassmorphism, heavy shadows, non-functional bright colors, or gratuitous animation.
Don't go consumer/playful: no pastels, mascot illustrations, emoji, or casual copy.
Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't introduce a second typeface for hierarchy.
Don't hardcode a hex or px value in any component; add a token first.
